Ah, got it! You want to build your own agents using the Gemini API. 

The backend portal where you can generate API keys to use the Gemini models is **Google AI Studio**. 

Here is the direct link to get your API keys:
**🔗 [https://aistudio.google.com/](https://aistudio.google.com/)**

*(If you are building an enterprise application, you can also use **[Google Cloud Vertex AI](https://console.cloud.google.com/vertex-ai)**, which ties directly into your GCP account).*

### How many "agents" can you make, and can they run simultaneously?
**Yes, they can run simultaneously!** You can build and spawn as many AI agents as you want. When you write a script to call the Gemini API, you can have 10, 100, or 1,000 agents running at the exact same time.

The only limit to how many you can run simultaneously is your **API Quota** (Rate Limits), which measures:
1. **RPM (Requests Per Minute):** How many times your agents can ping the API in 60 seconds.
2. **TPM (Tokens Per Minute):** How much text/data they can process per minute.

**In Google AI Studio:**
- There is a **Free Tier** that gives you a generous amount of RPM and TPM to start building and testing multiple agents simultaneously.
- You can set up billing (Pay-as-you-go) to massively increase those rate limits if you need to run a huge swarm of agents at the same time. 

So if you want to build an army of OSINT agents that all scrape, analyze, and process data at the exact same time using your API key, you absolutely can do that!

The objective is to back up our complete chat history, my system directions, and our operating state to multiple redundant locations as requested, while following the new safety and backup rules.

We will execute the backup across three main vectors: Local, Git/GitHub, and Google Drive.

> [!IMPORTANT]

### 3. Google Drive Backup
> [!WARNING]

To get this to your "sharedall" folder on GDrive, we have two options:
1. **Manual Drop (Recommended for speed):** Once I generate the local backup file in the step above, you can drag and drop it into your "sharedall" Google Drive folder.
2. **Python Script:** I can write a Python script using the Google Drive API to upload it automatically, provided you have the Drive API enabled in your GCP project and the credentials have the correct scopes.

### 4. BigQuery Tracking
I will insert a record into `noble-beanbag-497411-m4.ai_sandbox.ag_status_tracker` documenting the exact timestamp and file locations of this full backup.

## Open Questions

1. Will you handle the Google Drive upload manually by dropping the file into "sharedall", or do you want me to attempt writing a Python script to do it?

"# Mission State & Chat Backup Plan

The objective is to back up our complete chat history, my system directions, and our operating state to multiple redundant locations as requested, while following the new safety and backup rules.

## Proposed Changes

We will execute the backup across three main vectors: Local, Git/GitHub, and Google Drive.

### 1. Local Workspace Backup
I will copy the raw internal chat transcript and system instructions log to a dedicated backup file inside your `osint-agent` directory.
#### [NEW] [ag_mission_backup.jsonl](file:///C:/Users/HP/.gemini/antigravity-ide/scratch/osint-agent/ag_mission_backup.jsonl)

### 2. GitHub Backup & Privacy
I will commit the local backup file to your local `git` repository and push it to the `Tonypost949/osint-agent` remote.

> [!IMPORTANT]
> The GitHub CLI is not installed on this machine, so I cannot automatically change the repository visibility. You will need to manually log into GitHub and change the repository to **Private** in the repository settings to fulfill the privacy requirement.

### 3. Google Drive Backup
> [!WARNING]
> Google Cloud CLI (`gcloud`) does not have native commands for uploading to Google Drive.

To get this to your "sharedall" folder on GDrive, we have two options:
1. **Manual Drop (Recommended for speed):** Once I generate the local backup file in the step above, you can drag and drop it into your "sharedall" Google Drive folder.
2. **Python Script:** I can write a Python script using the Google Drive API to upload it automatically, provided you have the Drive API enabled in your GCP project and the credentials have the correct scopes.

### 4. BigQuery Tracking
I will insert a record into `noble-beanbag-497411-m4.ai_sandbox.ag_status_tracker` documenting the exact timestamp and file locations of this full backup.

## Open Questions

1. Will you handle the Google Drive upload manually by dropping the file into "sharedall", or do you want me to attempt writing a Python script to do it?
